response:
# Dark Mode Toggle Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only users, low vision users, users with contrast sensitivity, users in forced-colors environments > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, keyboard-only navigation, Windows forced-colors mode **Feature:** Add an accessible dark mode toggle to the top navigation, apply system dark preference on first load, persist user choice in `localStorage`, and validate dark-mode design tokens. **Risk Level:** Medium **Component/Page Type:** Icon-only toggle button, theme state persistence, visual token system --- ## Scope & Context This plan covers three related but distinct accessibility concerns: 1. A top-navigation icon-only theme toggle. 2. Initial theme selection using `prefers-color-scheme: dark`. 3. Contrast validation for light and dark token pairs, including focus indicators and non-text UI boundaries. The implementation must keep the existing React architecture, CSS custom properties, and `data-theme` attribute on ``. The design must not conflate dark mode with forced-colors mode. `prefers-color-scheme: dark` expresses a user color-scheme preference; `forced-colors: active` is a separate operating-system contrast mode where the browser may override author colors. This plan is not claiming that dark mode automatically improves accessibility. It only defines how to implement dark mode without reducing accessibility. Any information currently conveyed by color alone in light mode remains a WCAG 1.4.1 issue in dark mode unless a non-color indicator is added. --- ## Semantic Structure Plan The toggle belongs in the existing top navigation because theme selection is a global site preference. It should be implemented as a native `
``` Semantic decisions: | Area | Plan | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---| | Toggle control | Native `button` because it performs an action in the current page | 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value | | Navigation placement | Keep inside primary `nav`; it is a global site control | 1.3.1 Info and Relationships | | Icon | Decorative icon marked `aria-hidden="true"` because the accessible name comes from the button | 1.1.1 Non-text Content, 4.1.2 | | Skip link | Preserve or add skip link before navigation | 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks | | Theme state | Reflect current theme on `` | 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 4.1.2 where state affects controls | --- ## Interaction Pattern Design ### Pattern Choice Use the **APG Button Pattern: Toggle Button**, not `role="switch"`. Specification: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ Justification: the control changes a presentation preference for the site. A native button with `aria-pressed` communicates a pressed/unpressed toggle state without replacing native button semantics. `role="switch"` could also be defensible for binary settings, but it creates a custom widget expectation and requires careful naming around “on/off.” For this existing icon-only nav control, `button` + `aria-pressed` is simpler, native, and sufficient. Important naming rule: the accessible name should not change with state if using `aria-pressed`. Use a stable name such as `aria-label="Use dark mode"` or `aria-label="Dark mode"`, while `aria-pressed` communicates whether it is active. Preferred accessible name: ```text aria-label="Dark mode" aria-pressed="true | false" ``` Expected screen reader announcement: - Light mode active, dark mode off: “Dark mode, toggle button, not pressed” - Dark mode active: “Dark mode, toggle button, pressed” Do not use `title` as the accessible name. If a tooltip is desired, it must not be the only naming mechanism. ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA Attributes | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Dark mode toggle | APG Button, toggle button: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ | `Tab` focuses button; `Enter` activates; `Space` activates | `aria-label="Dark mode"`, `aria-pressed="true/false"` | 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.3 Focus Order, 4.1.2 Name Role Value | | Skip link | Native link | `Tab` focuses; `Enter` jumps to main content | `href="#main-content"` | 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks, 2.1.1 Keyboard | | Existing nav links | Native links | `Tab` focuses; `Enter` navigates | Descriptive link text | 2.4.4 Link Purpose, 4.1.2 | ### Toggle Behavior On first load: 1. If a valid persisted value exists in `localStorage`, apply that value. 2. Otherwise, if `prefers-color-scheme: dark` matches, apply dark mode. 3. Otherwise, apply light mode. 4. After the theme is applied, synchronize: - `` - visual icon - `aria-pressed` - any internal React state The live DOM state is authoritative for accessibility. `aria-pressed` must reflect the active rendered theme, not a stale `localStorage` value. --- ## Focus Management Plan No focus trap is required because this is not a modal, drawer, popover, or composite widget. Focus order: 1. Skip link. 2. Primary navigation links in visual order. 3. Dark mode toggle at its visual position in the nav. 4. Main content links and controls. Focus behavior: | Scenario | Focus Plan | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---| | User tabs to toggle | Focus lands on the native button in visual nav order | 2.4.3 Focus Order | | User activates toggle | Focus remains on the toggle; do not move focus | 2.4.3 Focus Order | | Theme changes | Focus indicator must remain visible after colors update | 2.4.7 Focus Visible, 2.4.13 Focus Appearance | | Page loads with system preference | Do not move focus; apply theme before or during initial render | 3.2.2 On Input, 2.4.3 | | `localStorage` unavailable | Fall back to system preference or light mode without breaking the button | 4.1.2 Name Role Value | The existing `2px solid #0066CC` focus outline must be re-evaluated in dark mode. It may pass against light backgrounds but fail against dark surfaces or saturated dark tokens. Plan a two-color focus indicator: ```text Outer/inner focus treatment: - One dark color and one light color, or system colors in forced-colors mode. - Minimum 3:1 contrast between focused and unfocused states. - Minimum visible area equivalent to at least a 2px perimeter. ``` Use `:focus-visible` for the author focus style. Ensure the button has a stable target size of at least 24x24 CSS px, with 44x44 CSS px preferred. --- ## State Communication Design ### State Communication Table | State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA Attribute | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Light mode active | Light token set; sun/moon icon state | ``; toggle not pressed | `aria-pressed="false"` | 4.1.2 Name Role Value | | Dark mode active | Dark token set; icon changes | ``; toggle pressed | `aria-pressed="true"` | 4.1.2 Name Role Value | | Toggle focused | Visible focus ring in current mode | Browser/author focus state | none required beyond native focus | 2.4.7 Focus Visible, 2.4.13 Focus Appearance | | Toggle disabled, if ever needed | Muted visual style plus unavailable affordance | Native `disabled` if truly unavailable | Avoid `aria-disabled` on native button unless keeping it focusable intentionally | 4.1.2 | | Theme applying | Optional short transition; no content change requiring announcement | No live region needed | none | 4.1.3 Status Messages | | `localStorage` read failure | Theme still usable for current session | Button state reflects live theme | `aria-pressed` remains synchronized | 4.1.2 | | Forced-colors active | System colors override tokens | CSS `@media (forced-colors: active)` | none required | 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast | Do not add an `aria-live` announcement for routine theme toggling. The changed `aria-pressed` state on the focused button is sufficient and less noisy. If a visual toast such as “Dark mode enabled” is added later, it must use one shared polite status region, not repeated alert regions. Icon handling: - The sun/moon icon is decorative because `aria-label` and `aria-pressed` provide the accessible name and state. - Mark the icon `aria-hidden="true"`. - If the icon uses `+`, `-`, moon glyphs, pseudo-elements, or an icon font, prevent redundant screen reader announcements. --- ## Visual Accessibility Plan ### Contrast Requirements Dark mode tokens are drafted but unvalidated. They must not ship until token pairs are measured in both light and dark themes. Minimum ratios: | Token Pair / UI Area | Required Ratio | WCAG Citation | |---|---:|---| | Normal text on page background | 4.5:1 | 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum | | Normal text on elevated surfaces | 4.5:1 | 1.4.3 | | Large text, at least 18pt or 14pt bold equivalent | 3:1 | 1.4.3 | | Icons that convey function, including toggle icon | 3:1 against adjacent background | 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast | | Button boundaries, input borders, control outlines needed to identify components | 3:1 | 1.4.11 | | Focus indicator against adjacent colors | 3:1 | 2.4.7 Focus Visible, 2.4.13 Focus Appearance | | Link text against body text when links are distinguished only partly by color | 3:1 between link and surrounding text, plus non-color cue in prose | 1.4.1 Use of Color | Contrast validation matrix: | Theme | Pair | Must Validate | |---|---|---| | Light | `text.primary` on `background.page` | 4.5:1 | | Light | `text.secondary` on `background.page` | 4.5:1 unless purely decorative | | Light | `link.default` on `background.page` | 4.5:1 and link distinction | | Light | `button.icon` on nav background | 3:1 | | Light | `focus.outline` on nav/page/elevated surfaces | 3:1 | | Dark | `text.primary` on `background.page` | 4.5:1 | | Dark | `text.secondary` on `background.page` | 4.5:1 unless not essential | | Dark | `link.default` on `background.page` | 4.5:1 and link distinction | | Dark | `button.icon` on nav background | 3:1 | | Dark | `border.subtle` on dark surfaces, if needed to identify components | 3:1 | | Dark | `focus.outline`, including current `#0066CC`, on all dark surfaces | 3:1 | The existing `#0066CC` focus outline is a known risk. It must be tested against: - Dark page background. - Dark nav background. - Dark elevated card/surface background. - Dark interactive button background. - Adjacent icon/button colors. If it fails any common surface, replace it with a tokenized focus style that works independently in light and dark mode. ### Forced-Colors Plan Forced-colors is not dark mode. It requires separate CSS and testing. Plan: ```css @media (forced-colors: active) { /* Use system colors for controls, borders, and focus indicators. */ } ``` Requirements: - Do not rely on dark-mode custom properties inside forced-colors mode. - Preserve visible focus using system colors such as `Highlight`. - Do not remove outlines. - Avoid background images or icon-only color fills as the sole indicator. - Test with Windows High Contrast / forced-colors active separately from `prefers-color-scheme`. ### Motion and Transitions Theme transitions are allowed only if subtle and non-essential. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` by disabling or minimizing transitions. WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions. ### Target Size The icon-only button must have a minimum target size of 24x24 CSS px under WCAG 2.5.8, with 44x44 CSS px preferred for usability. --- ## Content Accessibility Plan The toggle is icon-only visually, so the accessible name is mandatory. Content rules: | Element | Plan | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---| | Toggle button | Stable accessible name: `aria-label="Dark mode"` | 4.1.2 | | Sun/moon icon | Decorative; `aria-hidden="true"` | 1.1.1 | | Tooltip, if present | Supplemental only; never the sole accessible name | 4.1.2 | | Documentation links | Body links remain underlined or otherwise non-color distinguished in both modes | 1.4.1, 2.4.4 | | Page language | Preserve `` while adding `data-theme` | 3.1.1 | | Reading order | Theme toggle must not visually move away from DOM order at responsive breakpoints | 1.3.2, 2.4.3 | The label should not be “Toggle dark mode” if the implementation relies on `aria-pressed`, because some screen readers may announce “Toggle dark mode, toggle button, pressed,” which is awkward and less clear. Prefer “Dark mode.” --- ## Testing Strategy ### Automated and Static Testing Use static checks for invalid ARIA and runtime checks for rendered state. Automated checks: - Validate no invalid ARIA attributes. - Validate the icon-only button has an accessible name. - Validate color contrast in rendered light and dark states. - Validate no focusable element is hidden or visually unreachable. - Validate `aria-pressed` appears on the button, not on a wrapper. DOM verification is required. It is not enough to test React state or visual theme changes. ### Keyboard Testing Test cases: - `Tab` reaches the skip link first. - `Tab` reaches the dark mode button in logical nav order. - Focus indicator is visible in light mode. - Press `Space`; theme changes; focus remains on button; `aria-pressed` updates. - Press `Enter`; theme changes back; focus remains on button; `aria-pressed` updates. - Reload page; persisted theme is applied; `aria-pressed` matches the rendered theme. - Clear `localStorage`; set system preference to dark; first load applies dark theme and `aria-pressed="true"`. ### Screen Reader Testing NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver scenarios: - On focus, button is announced with name, role, and pressed state. - In light mode: expected announcement equivalent to “Dark mode, toggle button, not pressed.” - In dark mode: expected announcement equivalent to “Dark mode, toggle button, pressed.” - Icon is not separately announced. - Toggle activation does not cause noisy or duplicate live-region announcements. ### Visual Testing Test matrix: | Test | Light | Dark | Forced Colors | |---|---:|---:|---:| | Text contrast | Required | Required | Browser/system colors verified | | Link distinction in prose | Required | Required | Required | | Toggle icon contrast | Required | Required | Required | | Button target size | Required | Required | Required | | Focus indicator contrast | Required | Required, especially against dark nav | Required | | 200% zoom | Required | Required | Recommended | | 400% zoom / 320px equivalent | Required for layout | Required for layout | Recommended | | Reduced motion | Required if transitions exist | Required if transitions exist | Not a substitute | Acceptance criteria: - All text token pairs meet WCAG 1.4.3. - All meaningful icon, border, and focus token pairs meet WCAG 1.4.11 or 2.4.7/2.4.13 as applicable. - `aria-pressed` always matches the live `data-theme`. - Forced-colors mode remains usable without relying on dark-mode variables. --- ## Implementation Tasks ### Task 1: Theme Toggle Semantics 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Theme toggle component. - Top navigation component if the toggle is composed there. **Structure Stub:** ```html ``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-label="Dark mode"`: provides accessible name for icon-only button. WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-pressed="true | false"`: communicates toggle state. WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-hidden="true"` on icon: prevents duplicate icon announcement. WCAG 1.1.1. **Keyboard Interactions:** - `Tab`: focus button. - `Enter`: toggle theme. - `Space`: toggle theme. - Focus remains on button after activation. **Tests:** - Button has accessible name. - Icon is not announced as separate content. - `aria-pressed` changes on `Space` and `Enter`. - Focus remains visible and stable after activation. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.1.1 Keyboard. - 2.4.3 Focus Order. - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. - 1.1.1 Non-text Content. --- ### Task 2: Theme Resolution and Persistence 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Theme state utility/hook. - Initial theme bootstrapping logic. - Root document integration that sets ``. **Behavior Plan:** 1. Read persisted user choice. 2. If valid, use it. 3. Else use `prefers-color-scheme: dark`. 4. Else default to light. 5. Update ``. 6. Update React state. 7. Update toggle `aria-pressed`. **Accessibility Requirements:** - `localStorage` must not be treated as the accessibility source of truth. - The rendered DOM must be internally consistent: - `data-theme="dark"` means `aria-pressed="true"`. - `data-theme="light"` means `aria-pressed="false"`. **Tests:** - First load with no storage and dark system preference applies dark. - First load with persisted light overrides dark system preference. - Toggle updates DOM state and storage. - Storage failure does not break keyboard or ARIA behavior. **WCAG Criteria:** - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. - 3.2.2 On Input. - 2.4.3 Focus Order. --- ### Task 3: Dark Token Contrast Validation 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Token documentation or test fixture. - Contrast test configuration. - CSS custom properties for light and dark tokens. **Required Validation:** - Text on page background. - Text on elevated/card surfaces. - Link text on backgrounds and against surrounding text where relevant. - Toggle icon against nav background. - Interactive borders against adjacent surfaces. - Focus indicator against all major backgrounds. **Tests:** - Programmatic contrast checks for required token pairs. - Runtime contrast scan in light mode. - Runtime contrast scan in dark mode. - Manual review of any semantic color states: error, warning, success, disabled. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum. - 1.4.1 Use of Color. - 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast. - 2.4.7 Focus Visible. - 2.4.13 Focus Appearance. --- ### Task 4: Focus Indicator and Forced-Colors Support 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Global focus styles. - Theme token CSS. - Forced-colors CSS overrides. **Plan:** - Replace or validate `#0066CC` as a tokenized focus color. - Use a two-color focus indicator where possible. - Use `:focus-visible`. - Add `@media (forced-colors: active)` rules for focus, borders, and icon visibility. - Do not treat forced-colors as a dark theme variant. **Tests:** - Focus ring visible on nav background in light mode. - Focus ring visible on nav background in dark mode. - Focus ring visible on elevated dark surfaces. - Forced-colors mode shows usable system-color focus. - No outline suppression. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.4.7 Focus Visible. - 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured. - 2.4.13 Focus Appearance. - 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast. --- ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus Areas | |---|---|---| | 🔍 1 | Task 1 | Verify APG toggle button pattern, stable accessible name, correct `aria-pressed`, decorative icon handling | | 🔍 2 | Task 2 | Verify `data-theme`, React state, storage, and `aria-pressed` never diverge | | 🔍 3 | Task 3 | Verify dark token contrast matrix covers text, links, borders, icons, and focus indicators | | 🔍 4 | Task 4 | Verify focus indicator passes in both themes and forced-colors is handled as a separate mechanism | --- ## Failure Modes This plan specifically prevents: - Using an icon-only button without an accessible name. - Using `title` instead of `aria-label`. - Changing the visual theme while leaving `aria-pressed` stale. - Treating `localStorage` as more authoritative than the live DOM. - Validating contrast only in light mode. - Assuming inverted colors pass WCAG contrast. - Shipping the existing `#0066CC` focus outline without testing it on dark surfaces. - Conflating `prefers-color-scheme` with `forced-colors`. - Treating color-only information as fixed by dark mode. ## References - WAI-ARIA APG Button Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ - WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/ - MDN `prefers-color-scheme`: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@media/prefers-color-scheme - MDN `forced-colors`: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@media/forced-colors
